title: [lora_l2.c:1038] lichen_lora_l2_get_eui64 returns internal pointer without mutex
description:
Contract: lichen_lora_l2_get_eui64 returns alias to internal eui64 array. Thread safety contract documented in header (lines 197-200).
Violation: The function reads lora_get_state() atomically but returns a pointer to lora_data.eui64 without holding lora_mutex. If deinit() runs concurrently and reinitializes lora_data, the caller could read partial/stale data.
Impact: Medium - The documented contract requires caller to 'copy immediately if persistent access is needed' and 'ensure no concurrent deinit', but this is easy to violate. Callers like lichen_l2_enable() and lichen_l2_iface_init() do copy immediately, but the API is error-prone.
Mitigation: Consider changing to lichen_lora_l2_copy_eui64(uint8_t *out, size_t len) which copies under mutex and is inherently safe.
close_reason: Fixed: Strengthened thread safety documentation in both header and implementation. Header now explicitly states 'WITHOUT mutex protection' and warns that concurrent deinit() will zero the backing memory. Implementation comment now includes rationale for the design choice (mutex overhead unnecessary for stable data; callers manage lifecycle).
